SourceProxy.name: SHYXVmtkOpeningCenterlines
SourceProxy.label: SHYX VMTK Opening Centerlines

Documentation.short_help: Threshold openings, pick inlets/outlets, optional VMTK centerlines.

SourceProxy.Documentation:
Single surface input. Cells whose chosen scalar magnitude lies in [Threshold min, Threshold max] (same point/cell resolution rules as SHYX Adaptive Isotropic Remesher remesh range) are extracted; each connected component defines one opening. The representative point is the mesh vertex closest to that patch centroid (for disk-like inlets/outlets).
The **Inlets (openings)** list is filled after Apply. Each row is keyed as **SeedPoint: <SurfacePointId>** (representative surface vertex id); identical ids get **#2**, **#3** suffixes. Check entries that are **inlets** (VMTK source seeds); unchecked openings are **outlets** (targets). Turn **Calculate centerline** on only after marking inlets so centerlines are not computed during exploration.
The **Remove openings** list mirrors each discovery row; checked openings are skipped on output port 1 (seed points) and excluded from centerline seeds (treated as deleted). Assign **Inlets** only among openings that remain unchecked here.
Changing **Threshold array**, **min/max**, or **all corners** clears prior inlet/outlet and remove checks and rebuilds the opening list on the next Apply/update.
Output 0 is VMTK centerline polydata when **Calculate centerline** is on and at least one checked inlet and one unchecked outlet exist among non-removed openings; otherwise empty (the filter shallow-copies the input internally for VMTK with point GlobalIds).
Output 1 is one vertex per **non-removed** opening with arrays OpeningArrayValue, OpeningIndex, SurfacePointId, IsInlet; **SurfacePointId** is the active scalars array for display.
Requires VMTK compatible with this ParaView VTK (same as SHYX VMTK Centerlines).

InputProperty.name: Input
InputProperty.Documentation: Vessel lumen surface with cell-centered scalars for thresholding openings (picker lists cell arrays only so the default EndpointIndex resolves to cell data when a point array of the same name also exists).

StringVectorProperty.name: ThresholdArrayName
StringVectorProperty.label: Threshold array
StringVectorProperty.Documentation: Cell-centered array on the input (same VTK input-array slot as other SHYX filters). Default: **EndpointIndex** on **Cell Data** (e.g. vtkCGALVesselEndClipper caps). The array list is restricted to cell arrays so ParaView does not pick a same-named point array by default when both exist.

DoubleVectorProperty.name: ThresholdMin
DoubleVectorProperty.label: Threshold min
DoubleVectorProperty.Documentation: Inclusive lower bound on tuple magnitude for thresholded cells. Slider range follows the selected array’s actual min/max (information pipeline).

DoubleVectorProperty.name: ThresholdMax
DoubleVectorProperty.label: Threshold max
DoubleVectorProperty.Documentation: Inclusive upper bound; must be >= Threshold min. Slider range follows the selected array’s actual min/max (information pipeline).

IntVectorProperty.name: ThresholdAllScalars
IntVectorProperty.label: Threshold: all corners (points)
IntVectorProperty.Documentation: When the threshold array is point-centered: if ON, every corner of a cell must fall inside the range; if OFF, one in-range corner is enough. Ignored for cell-centered arrays.

StringVectorProperty.name: InletStatus
StringVectorProperty.label: Inlets (openings)
StringVectorProperty.Documentation: One entry per connected component after thresholding. Apply once to populate the list. Checked = inlet (VMTK source seed); unchecked = outlet (target). Names reflect threshold array magnitudes (mean per patch). Needs at least one of each when Calculate centerline is on.

StringVectorProperty.name: ExcludedStatus
StringVectorProperty.label: Remove openings
StringVectorProperty.Documentation: Checked openings are omitted from output seed points and from centerline source/target seeds (treated as deleted). Unchecked openings remain available for inlet/outlet assignment.

IntVectorProperty.name: CalculateCenterline
IntVectorProperty.label: Calculate centerline
IntVectorProperty.Documentation: When off (default), only seed glyphs (port 1) and the opening lists are produced. When on, runs vtkvmtkPolyDataCenterlines using non-removed checked openings as sources and non-removed unchecked as targets.
